# open-dictate

> Free, open-source voice dictation for Windows. Powered by Whisper, runs locally, and is inspired by the original open-wispr for macOS.

open-dictate is a lightweight Windows app that provides push-to-talk voice dictation. Hold a hotkey, speak, release, and transcribed text appears at your cursor in any app. It uses Whisper locally, so the intended workflow keeps audio and text on-device.

## Key Facts

- **Price**: Free and open source
- **Platform**: Windows 10 and Windows 11
- **Privacy**: Local transcription on your machine. Audio recordings are only kept if `maxRecordings` is configured above `0`
- **Install (recommended)**: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "Invoke-Expression ((New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tucaz/open-dictate/main/scripts/install.ps1'))"`
- **Install (manual ZIP)**: Download `open-dictate-windows-vX.X.X.zip`, extract it, then run `install.ps1` from the extracted folder
- **Install (portable)**: Download `open-dictate-windows-vX.X.X.zip`, extract it, then run `open-dictate.exe start`
- **Default start command**: `open-dictate start`
- **Source**: https://github.com/tucaz/open-dictate
- **Original macOS project**: https://open-wispr.com/

## How It Works

1. Hold the configured hotkey (Right Ctrl by default)
2. Speak naturally
3. Release and the text is inserted at your active cursor

## Features

- Works across apps wherever the active text cursor is available
- Configurable hotkey, language, model size, spoken punctuation, and recording retention
- Multiple Whisper model sizes: `tiny.en`, `tiny`, `base.en`, `base`, `small.en`, `small`, `medium.en`, `medium`, `large`
- Multilingual support with Whisper language codes such as `pt`, `es`, `fr`, `de`, and `ja`
- Recommended PowerShell installer plus portable and manual ZIP paths
- Windows rewrite of the original Swift/macOS project

## Configuration

Config file location: `%APPDATA%\open-dictate\config.json`

Default configuration:

```json
{
  "hotkey": { "keyCode": 163, "modifiers": [] },
  "modelSize": "base.en",
  "language": "en",
  "spokenPunctuation": false,
  "maxRecordings": 0
}
```

For non-English dictation, switch from `base.en` to a multilingual model such as `base` or `small`, then set the matching language code in the config.

Example:

```powershell
open-dictate download-model base
open-dictate set-model base
```

Then update config:

```json
{
  "modelSize": "base",
  "language": "pt"
}
```

## System Requirements

- Windows 10 or Windows 11
- Around 150 MB disk space for the default `base.en` model
- Rough Whisper memory guidance: about 1 GB for `tiny`/`base`, 2 GB for `small`, 5 GB for `medium`, and 10 GB for `large`
- `base.en` or `base` is the sensible default for most machines; `small` is the next step up if you want better accuracy
